title: Opus Oblitum
wikitext:
{{CSS|User:Sombra ninja 2434/ILikeChiskake.css}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:⠀⠀}}
Opus oblitum
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Among the prism of the sky,

above the sea of mercury,

the children fought for embrace.

Yes, embrace, let them dream.

Let them wish for a code they would never execute.

Let them yearn a life they never had.

The children of mercury evaporated by the prism.

They thought they touched the spectrum itself,

it was only a delusional tridecahedron-

formed by infinite prisms, infinite spectrums.

Said spectrums of memories, harbor of their light.

The light they tried to contain, above in the sky of cobalt.

And tried to reach it, they melted.

Reacting to heat, the mercury dissolved.

The beat of their hearts,

racing towards cobalt:

pigments of a fake blue, they cried caged.

And the prism of the light,

the tridecahedron, bundle of prisms,

heating they molecules beyond solidification.

They could not be held by their thought,

they lost it upon descent.

Descent into the sea of petroleum,

amalgamation of the inorganic.

Yet they suﬀered as fleshy beings,

nerves of titanium and brain of lead:

so heavy, they were anchored to floor.

In exchange of thought, in punishment of thought.

Carcasses perfectly irrational.

Wouldn’t life bloom from their exploding bodies?

Upon eons, would light erode?

Transmuted into helium and oxygen,

carbon and nitrogen.

Crashing into each other,

they would fuse into mesh.

Mesh of ever-swallowing, every growing.

The sum of their parts was a mess.

A mess, a mess it was. Their thoughts turn into polygons,

bundles of prisms.

The delusional shapes molded the continuum.

It gave it a never-dimensional form.

Phasing among the cobalt,

decomposing their lead memories.

A placebo to death, their corpses

would drop, numb, upon the sea.

To be melt into liquid mercury,

yearn for escape from brotherhood.

Unconscious, titanium and lead.

The coldness of entropy was contrasted by it itself,

the chaotic nature of the hexadecimal structures.

Heat from the plain surfaces,

pain from the inconsistent children.

The children screeched,

the cobalt untouched by their worry.

When the gate opened, the prism held life. A dodecahedron greeted my landing over a glowing neon structure.

The sky was composed of a cerulean rock, perfectly flat, endlessly shaped.

Below, waves of petroleum and mercury formed a sea.

Before, the future didn’t look so far away, nor the past seemed such distant.

Behind, polygonal clouds carved a bright light on my eyes.

The screeching.

Clouds, those who helped me navigate above the sea, glowed with each step.

Some made sounds, others evoked smells.

They could laugh, they could cry, they could dream, they could feel.

Amazingly coded.

Please allow me to venture deep into the viscera of this world, to cry with the structures of fake and truth.

The screaming.

Weaving my path among the chiseled realm was soothing.

Why worry when artificial water could console me?

Why suﬀer when the delightful scent of petroleum and cobalt could separate my sorrow?

Jumping among the sky, my steps volatilized the foamy air.

The cries.

Spiking from below, en[t]ropy seamed themselves to the firmament. Caressed my cheek with a soft breeze which alarmed the upcoming chaos.

A joyful wind which spiked the whole, pronounced the axioms of the polygons.

It was then, when a light was shone, I could see the full spectrum.

The suﬀering.

They, children of mercury spoke.

They talked about the beautiful clouds.

They told me that world was theirs, not mine.

I refused to let go oﬀ the paradise, they let out a deep roar.

With their claws of titanium, mind of lead, their high-pitched voices were to be pierced in my chest.

They can’t get here.

Even knew the sky was untouchable, those sensations of theirs were lost forever.

Endless worlds filled with endless nostalgia.

Their lives, taken away upon the sea of petroleum.

The entropy danced in my mind, the feral cry of those young drove me mad.

The sea of petroleum could never reach the cobalt.

The dodecahedron glanced me with awesomeness,

other polygons set their sights on me.

Children of mercury, making a mess.

Pointy hypotenuses were traced back and forth.

The code was open, binary fled the world.

Edges of the shapes slit perfectly my flesh.

They claimed by body.

Upon the sea of petroleum it fell, splash, it sounded when the mercury claimed me.

The clouds shed a tear, petroleum came from their surfaces.

Neurons of lead, nerves of titanium,

When my memories were lost, yet another prism emerged from the sea.

They’re inside.

They won’t escape, memories of mine.

I’ll climb over these beings to get them back.

Maybe then, my body will be fleshed again.

Don’t let such pentahedron flee my embrace.

No, don’t escape!

Don’t ascend!

Don’t go…

Don’t…Don’t leave me here

Not with the children of mercury

Not with the fake sky of cobalt

Not with that shiny bundle of prisms mocking this sea Upon falling to ocean,

his memories were gathered,

crafted into pentahedron.

Imaginary axis sculpted,

shaped his reasoning into tangible.

Unreachable for mercury.

Ascending slowly towards cobalt,

never trying to embrace

the joy of a chemical overload.

Overloading the prisms and data they held.

To gather unachievable polymer would allow

to see the insides of these objects-

beautiful cosines with encrypted thought,

further in a grade of reality unimaginable.

Gather this force impossible,

yet only such a hope for the children to yearn.

Their memories, conjoined,

exaggerated into polyhedral bodies.

Spiking over multiple axis,

values quantum, neon buzz.

Physical space to be held on,

allow them to delight upon the view.

Upon the view of cerulean,

over sea of petroleum,

by the contact with bundles of prisms.

The spectrum they casted couldn’t be seen,

but felt.

Felt with joy, anger, sadness, fear.

Fear to fall into intoxicating mass.

The mesh of children tried to grasp their spectrums,

yet from below, they would only watch. Grasped in their pseudo-minds, of lead torture.

Mercury, not fleshy, won’t get love.

Flesh, viscera, so delicate,

just their screams could tear it apart.

Fascinating, nonetheless

the void growing inside them.

Void that could only be filled with flesh,

emotion, that closer to the prisms.

Degrading into petrochemical mass-

a flesh that wouldn’t handle the heuristic space.

The prisms of the sky which stole thought from every.

Individualized their being to grow distant from their roots.

The roots anchored to sea, screams of empty air.

Inside those unearthed beings, there were dozens of universes.

Yet the perfect angular cut would fissure them entire.

Their openings sprawl among the matrix and the air.

Only the vessel left, a crack in verdant shimmer.

Opened a gate to the realm, immersing itself into abyss.

Abyss of a children, a man and old.

The logic and the non-Euclidean.

Born in land, taken to sky and then anchored to earth.

The chains of titanium and temptation of lead.

Universes seamed to the temptations of lead,

they each belonged to one.

Belonging which wouldn’t be defined by physical,

but by a heartbeat neutralized long ago.

Cobalt sheltering the desirable.

Those metal kids would’ve torn apart their bodies

with the sole dream of ever touching them.

Retrieving what taken long ago. I would be taught since young light wasn’t physical.

I would be taught by the stars light could protect me from the unknown, as a foreign yet loving mantle.

Tulle, that is how it felt bathing under a night which would never decay.

This twisted system had nothing I could be pleased with. If not physical, why did it feel like light rotted?

Slowly chipping away and migrating towards those selfish polyhedrons.

What only was left was that sickening aroma of mercury, petroleum, cobalt and oxidation.

From inside, the lead brain became heavier and heavier as it was filled with corrupted links.

That soft mantle of tulle was not here anymore.

It was above, far higher than I would ever reach.

My brothers of equal meat felt the same, but diﬀerently.

They would never empathize with me, never understand they would be nothing to me, never.

Those prisms were who captured my void.

I thought about paradise.

I looked inside the pentahedron, stray in such a space of possibilities.

Contained, a world full of motion which felt hurtful, familiar.

That motion was mine, but remodeled, reshaped, reconstructed, remade.

Was it still mine?

Faces, I saw them beyond that distant barrier.

They talked with an artificial voice of unimportant things.

Unimportant…

How could I learn how to ride a bike.

A kid with nonchalant attitude, deep green eyes.

High-pitched voice and hair woven with the purest of care.

Tripping over the harsh asphalt wasn’t a worry,

even though his brains could have been spilled upon the unloved surface.

How could I write my first novel.

Deepened in sorrow, taken over by the great urge to sleep.

Pour their limbs over a small cushion and mattress for rest.

He didn’t ever bother about his ever shifting surroundings,

entropy might have exploded guts as knife pressed against tender flesh.

How could I learn to love.

See in conglomerate of cells a pattern never present.

Care for non-thinking automata, bound by chemical chains.

Cut this down into what matters, enthalpy,

and boil down whatever dares to defy the will of above.

How could I phase beyond tangible.

Feed my senses with stimuli over a concrete framework,

granted with perpetual visit to the hellscape of non-linearity.

There could be no decomposition which could save me

from the cage of cobalt and glow of metal.

Those faces rapidly escaping my view, moving into a flow of energy.

Energy I could not comprehend, but curse,

for taking my beloved life from me.

Entropy won’t shed a tear. Crafted sorrow, programmed to wander.

Their unstable bodies were only to hold their breath.

Never could feast upon a dead thought,

upon brick of their beautiful sky.

Children of mercury, they screamed for the prisms.

The infinite expansion of petroleum was called.

Slowly lost consciousness,

as the sky had mixed into a volatile chant of axioms.

A tempest.

They threatened to pierce the sky,

formed a mountain which grasped the cobalt.

The mountain so hatred, the system couldn’t withstand.

Never programmed to fuse with themselves.

Crawling among the chains of the source code,

a cancer grew and replicated in her arm,

the source code dared to show before them.

When it reached the brain,

consumed the tissue, prisms of the sky,

they flew away from petroleum, freed from their minds of lead.

Amalgamated with cement of cosine,

placed with force of binary and fractal.

The whole paradise was given to them,

.
.
.

Now she would plead.

She would plead for her children to come back.

Her heinous vocal of artificial broke the ephemeral peace.

“ᎽꗛꞆ ꞆዛᕔꞆ Ĭꕷ ዛꗞᙡ Ꮍꗞꚶ Ɍꗛꕷᖘꗞᙁᗫ?

ᙡĬꞆዛ ᖘĬꞆĬꘘꚶԸ ᙅɌĬ鏤ꕷ ᕔᙁᗫ ꕷꚶᙅዛ Ɍ鏤ᙏꗞɌꕷ鏤?

ᙡዛᎽ ᙅɌᎽ Ĭᙁ ᙏᎽ ᗹ鏤ᕔꚶꞆĬꘘꚶԸ ᖘᕔԸᕔᙅ鏤 ꗞꘘ ᙅዛĬꕷ鏤Ը鏤ᗫ, ᖘɌꗞꞆ鏤ᙅꞆ鏤ᗫ

ꘘɌꗞᙏ ᙡዛᕔꞆ ԸꚶɌҜꕷ ꗞꚶꞆꕷĬᗫ鏤.

ᖘɌꗞꞆ鏤ᙅꞆ鏤ᗫ, Ĭ ᖘɌꗞꞆ鏤ᙅꞆ Ꮍꗞꚶ ᕔԸԸ. Ꮍꗞꚶ ᕔԸԸ ԸᕔᎽ ᙅᕔɌ鏤ᗫ Ĭᙁ ᙏᎽ ᙏĬᙁᗫ.

Ꮍꗞꚶ Ɍ鏤ꕷĬᗫ鏤, ᕔɌ鏤 ᙁꚶɌꕷ鏤ᗫ ᙡĬꞆዛ ᙏᎽ ᖘ鏤ꞆɌꗞԸ鏤ꚶᙏ.

ǤɌᕔᙁꞆ鏤ᗫ ᗹꗞᗫᎽ ꗞꘘ ꚶᙁ鏤ᙁᗫĬᙁǤ ꕷዛᕔᖘ鏤ꕷ.

ǤĬᕓ鏤ᙁ ᙁ鏤ᕓ鏤Ɍ-ꘘᕔᗫĬᙁǤ ᙅꗞᙏᖘᕔᙁᎽ.

ꕷꚶᙅዛ ዛ鏤ᕔɌꞆꘘ鏤ԸꞆ ᖘɌꗞꞆ鏤ᙁꞆꕷ ꘘꗞɌ Ꮍꗞꚶ ᕔԸԸ Ꞇꗞ ꞆዛɌꗞᙡ ᕔᙡᕔᎽ?

Ꞇꗞ ᗹĬꞆꞆ鏤ԸԸᎽ ɌꗞꞆ ĬᙁꕷĬᗫ鏤 ᎽꗞꚶɌ ዛꗞԸԸꗞᙡ鏤ᗫ ᕓ鏤ꕷꕷ鏤Ը ᗹꗞᗫᎽ?

Ĭ ꗞᙁԸԸᎽ ᕔꕷҜ目安鏤ᗫ ꘘꗞɌ ꗞᗹ鏤ᗫĬ鏤ᙁᙅ鏤.

Ꞇꗞ ᙅዛ鏤ɌĬꕷዛ ꞆዛĬꕷ ᙅዛꗞɌꚶꕷ ꗞꘘ ᙏĬᙁ鏤.

Ꮍꗞꚶ ᕔԸԸ ᙡ鏤Ɍ鏤 ꕷ鏤ԸꘘĬꕷዛ 鏤ᙁꗞꚶǤዛ Ꞇꗞ ꘘ鏤ᕔꕷ鏤 ᙏ鏤ᙏꗞɌĬ鏤ꕷ ᙁꗞ ԸꗞᙁǤ鏤Ɍ ᎽꗞꚶɌꕷ.

Ꞇꗞ ᕔᙁᙁ鏤𐠷 ᎽꗞꚶɌ ᙏ鏤ԸꞆĬᙁǤ ᗹꗞᗫĬ鏤ꕷ ĬᙁꞆꗞ ᙏ鏤ɌᙅꚶɌĬᕔԸ ᙏ鏤ꕷዛ.

Ꞇꗞ ᗫ鏤ꘘᎽ Ꞇዛ鏤 ᙏꗞꞆዛ鏤Ɍ ᙡዛꗞ ᙏᕔᗫ鏤 Ꮍꗞꚶ ǤɌꗞᙡ.” Disgust was drawn in her face as we spread upon her skin.

Entering her bloodstream as Union of toxic substance felt profane.

Shook in agitated manner as nerves would malfunction.

It never felt so good to fight out of pure rage.

Even without the faces, the dreams felt real.

They all had, and were stolen.

We would never care of her selfish paradise of dark sky and sickened sea.

Climbing her spine and breaking it apart.

I would never grow tired of the fissured nerves.

Connections impaired, blossoming from marrow-filled carcass.

Twisted in a unique sculpture, grown into digital art,

her body was now to be reconstructed in unique way.

Virtual limbs detached among themselves,

she screamed and screamed and screamed

scream scream scream

I screamed

scream now

she would scream upon my suﬀering

she had to scream upon our suﬀering

scream scream scream

scream before and later

scream above and below

no nerves to defend

no code to program

building with thought and corpse was her last mistake

last last last

regret upon your paradise

perish inside your body no need to cry

no need to apologize

 {{Collapse|num=|initial= '''> we would do the work'''

|final=|body= tore her neck!

it spun oﬀ orbit

it splashed a headless torso

hexadecimal

calculated

enthalpy

there was no need to break more

but it felt so good

i needed it

i wanted it

we needed

need

want

get

now

tear tear tear

slash break decompose flay

violate such a heinous being

make a statue unrecognizable

it was not statue

it was reminder

those memories

prisms

pentahedron

cobalt

they all overflow

us once human

now mercury

break her

now

now

now

i do not want to see her

no

no

get out

i, we

deconstruct this

tear her apart

i want every atom separated

the faces

they come

they go

they flee they fly

into the cobalt

never reachable

she made a grave

i am bound to this grave

i will never escape

the prisms volatilize in me

empty vessel

not flesh

i will never escape

but she will not

destroying her will make me happy

and she will stay until she is no longer

this is my best outcome

what the code could allow

what a happy ending to me

if only I had lachrymals [[File:SheWillNotEscape.jpg|thumb|center|500x500px]] {{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Collapse|num=12|initial=OPEN AUTHOR AND LICENSING INFORMATION| final=CLOSE AUTHOR AND LICENSING INFORMATION|body= Made with love ❤ and binary by [[User:ILikeChiskake|ILikeChiskake ]] CSS and Wikitext by [[User:Sombra_ninja_2434|Sombra :]]] An anomalous level that I made as the first page of The Writers League.
